verbGlossary.SCOPED BY: {source}defines the project's element scope using {target} hierarchy (e.g., feasibility study scoped by UniFormat assemblies to establish system-level budgets)
verbGlossary.CATEGORIZED BY: {source} activity is classified using {target} multi-table taxonomy for facility type and space function (e.g., programming categorized by OmniClass tables for building use and space type)
verbGlossary.STRUCTURED BY: {source} work breakdown is organized according to {target} hierarchy (e.g., preconstruction plan structured by WBS codes, program structured by delivery method)
verbGlossary.SPECIFIED PER: {source} produces specifications that conform to {target} section format and content requirements (e.g., design documents specified per CSI specification sections)
verbGlossary.MODELED PER: {source}are classified according to the target schema during {target} (e.g., design model elements tagged per IFC classification for interoperability)
verbGlossary.DESIGNED TO: {source} incorporates {target} or standard's requirements into the building design (e.g., envelope designed to energy code thermal performance, layouts designed to ADA clearance requirements)
verbGlossary.QUANTIFIED BY: {source}measures quantities using {target} divisions as the takeoff structure (e.g., quantities taken off by MasterFormat division for trade-level pricing)
verbGlossary.CODED BY: {source} line items are assigned codes from {target} for cost tracking and reporting (e.g., estimate line items coded by standard cost codes)
verbGlossary.PRICED BY: {source}costs organized by {target} classification (e.g., conceptual estimate priced by UniFormat assembly for early-stage budgeting)
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKED TO: {source} cost or performance metric is compared to {target} published industry baselines (e.g., estimate benchmarked to AACE parametric data, punch item rates benchmarked to cost code standardization)
verbGlossary.ALLOCATED BY: {source} budget is distributed across {target} structure's line items (e.g., estimating budget allocated by budget codes to establish cost control baseline)
verbGlossary.REGULATED BY: {source} activity must satisfy {target} legal requirements before proceeding (e.g., permitting regulated by IBC occupancy and structural provisions, fire protection regulated by NFPA)
verbGlossary.PACKAGED BY: {source}groups scope into bid packages along {target} trade divisions (e.g., bid packages organized by MasterFormat divisions for subcontractor solicitation)
verbGlossary.CONTRACTED PER: {source} procurement uses contract forms and procedures from {target} (e.g., subcontracts executed per AIA contract documents during buyout)
verbGlossary.GOVERNED BY: {source} submittal requirements are dictated by the specification sections in {target} (e.g., submittals governed by specification section submittal requirements)
verbGlossary.FABRICATED PER: Fabrication work conforms to the material, tolerance, and workmanship requirements in the target {target} (e.g., structural steel fabricated per specification section welding and finishing requirements)
verbGlossary.TRACKED BY: {source}deliverable status during {target} is monitored using the target classification's divisions (e.g., material deliveries tracked by MasterFormat division for procurement status reporting)
verbGlossary.SAFEGUARDED PER: {source}setup phase implements hazard controls defined by {target} classification (e.g., perimeter protection and fall arrest installed per OSHA hazard category requirements)
verbGlossary.ADDRESSES: {source} orientation covers the hazard categories defined in {target} (e.g., safety orientation addresses OSHA Focus Four hazard categories for site-specific risks)
verbGlossary.DOCUMENTED PER: {source} records are created in the format required by {target} recordkeeping rules (e.g., safety orientation attendance documented per OSHA recordkeeping classification)
verbGlossary.TRAINED ON: {source}are trained during {target} on injury types and prevention methods from the target classification (e.g., orientation covers ANSI injury classifications for hazard recognition)
verbGlossary.INSPECTED PER: {source} work is inspected following the acceptance criteria and procedures in {target} (e.g., foundations inspected per IBC structural provisions, plumbing inspected per IBC plumbing code)
verbGlossary.TESTED PER: {source} installation undergoes testing protocols mandated by {target} (e.g., structural steel tested per specification section weld inspection, fire protection tested per NFPA)
verbGlossary.INSTALLED PER: {source} work is installed following the target {target} methods, materials, and tolerances (e.g., electrical rough-in installed per NFPA 70, flooring installed per specification section)
verbGlossary.RATED AGAINST: {source} assembly's performance is evaluated against {target} rating thresholds (e.g., roofing assembly rated against energy code R-value and solar reflectance requirements)
verbGlossary.COORDINATED BY: {source} trade sequencing is organized according to {target} division responsibilities (e.g., above-ceiling coordination sequenced by MasterFormat mechanical, electrical, and plumbing divisions)
verbGlossary.COMPLIES WITH: {source} material or process selection meets the target certification's environmental or health requirements (e.g., paint and coatings comply with LEED low-emitting materials credit)
verbGlossary.VERIFIED AGAINST: {source} phase test results are compared to {target} performance thresholds (e.g., pre-functional testing verified against energy code criteria, integrated testing verified against WELL)
verbGlossary.COMMISSIONED PER: Systems startup follows {target} commissioning and acceptance protocols (e.g., fire alarm startup commissioned per NFPA 72 acceptance testing procedures)
verbGlossary.BALANCED PER: Testing, adjusting, and balancing work follows the target {target} airflow, water flow, and sound criteria (e.g., HVAC systems balanced per specification section TAB requirements)
verbGlossary.CERTIFIED UNDER: {source} testing produces documentation required for the target sustainability certification (e.g., integrated testing certified under LEED energy performance prerequisites)
verbGlossary.VALIDATED AGAINST: {source}performance is confirmed against {target} (e.g., seasonal commissioning validated against net-zero energy targets)
verbGlossary.SEGMENTED BY: {source} metric is broken down by {target} mapping coverage for analysis (e.g., punch items segmented by MasterFormat mapping to identify problem divisions)
verbGlossary.MEASURED AGAINST: {source} outcome is evaluated relative to {target} completeness metric (e.g., owner training completeness measured against safety classification coverage)
verbGlossary.DELIVERED IN: {source} deliverables are formatted according to {target} schema (e.g., as-built models delivered in IFC classification format for facility management handoff)
verbGlossary.CLOSED PER: Contract closeout follows the procedures and documentation requirements in {target} standard (e.g., final payment and retainage released per AIA contract closeout provisions)
verbGlossary.RECONCILED AGAINST: {source} cost actuals are compared to {target} cost classification for final accounting (e.g., contract closeout costs reconciled against AACE cost engineering classifications)
verbGlossary.WARRANTED PER: Post-construction warranty obligations follow the target {target} warranty duration and coverage terms (e.g., roofing warranty period and scope warranted per specification section requirements)
verbGlossary.CATALOGED BY: {source}transition are classified using {target} product taxonomy (e.g., equipment and systems cataloged by OmniClass product tables for asset management)
